expectations.evidence: All required sections are present: Problem, Solution, Scope, Technical Approach, Risks, Alternatives, and Verification.
No matches for explicit file-line references like 'file.ts:123'.
No fenced code blocks tagged with ts/tsx/typescript/javascript/js are present.
Spec is 105 lines, below the 120-line limit.

Core behaviors are not fully actionable without follow-up: shortcut details in scope (notably exact `n` create behavior and some key-specific flows) are unresolved or left as clarification/open questions, so an implementer would need to ask before coding complete core flow.

Scope lists many distinct shortcuts (`n`, arrows, `Enter`, `Escape`, `d`, `p+1-4`, `/`, `?`), but Technical Approach does not provide explicit trigger/behavior mapping for each one; several are only implied.
First sentences of each Technical Approach paragraph clearly state major decisions (board-level orchestration, separate focus model, local filtering, interaction-mode precedence).
Uses formulaic self-referential phrasing at least 4 times (e.g., 'The non-obvious choice here is...', 'The design decision is...', 'The key decision is...'), exceeding the allowed threshold.

Technical Approach leaks implementation mechanics and code-level details (exact file paths, specific store fields like `activeTaskId`, `board?.columns`, and mode-resolution mechanics) rather than staying at behavior/policy level.
Three failure modes are included and each defines a policy choice (suspend during drag, visible expiring chord mode, deterministic focus re-anchoring fallback).
Out-of-scope items are specific and adjacent to likely creep: backend search APIs, custom keymaps, bulk actions, and app-wide global shortcuts.
